evidence_excerpt: Six months after the postoperative episode, she was free from haemorrhaging but the anti–von Willebrand factor antibody titre remained highly positive, indicating persistent inhibitor without clear resolution.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: Her bleeding phenotype is described as mild despite type 3 VWD, and she had no bleeding episodes for six months after the event.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: During the six-month interval after surgery, she did not receive any concentrate because she was free from haemorrhaging.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Following surgery, constant oozing of blood was reported, prompting higher doses and shorter intervals of rFVIIa.
Continuous infusion of recombinant FVIII (Advate) was initiated at high dose (≈20 IU/kg/h) after a bolus to achieve and maintain therapeutic FVIII activity.
Bleeding stopped within 4 hours after intensifying rFVIIa; subsequently there was no bleeding, and rFVIII infusion was discontinued on the 10th postoperative day.
A transfusion of red blood cells was administered on the third postoperative day.
Six months later, she was free from haemorrhaging and did not require any concentrate.

notes_on_uncertainty: Follow-up information extends to approximately six months after the index hospitalization; no data beyond 12 months are available. Functional status is inferred from clinician description of a mild bleeding phenotype and absence of bleeding episodes; specific activity or dependence details are not provided. The inhibitor remained highly positive at six months, but longer-term trajectory is not documented.
